violations.text: The sink and surface concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink was 3000 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of lactic acid in the sanitizing solution must be between 272-700 ppm. Sanitizer solution was corrected to 700 ppm at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2025
The sanitizer dispenser for the 3-bay sink was observed not working and in good repair. Equipment must be in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2025

Per interview with PIC, cheese sticks were delivered in coolers 20 minutes prior. Inspector observed no ice packs in coolers. Cheese sticks were observed at 47 degrees Fahrenheit. PIC moved cheese sticks to refrigeration at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025
Inspector observed deli meat dated (1/16 & 1/24) which exceeds 7 days. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

The sink and surface concentration in the sanitizing solution dispenser of the 3-bay sink was 0 parts per million (ppm). The concentration of lactic acid in the sanitizing solution must be between 0.27-0.55 ppm. PIC manually added. Service ticket will be submitted. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the sink and surface sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024
Hamburger buns were observed with green residue. See disposal. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with white and black debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2024

Potatoes were observed held at 117 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. New Violation. Correct By: 09/16/2024
There is insufficient hot holding equipment to maintain foods at 135 degrees F or above. New Violation. Correct By: 09/30/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back kitchen area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the: 1.shelves in kitchen 2.under benches in cafeteria have an accumulation of dust, dirt, rodent droppings, and other debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/01/2023
